frameworks.name: category-selective cortex / functional specialization
frameworks.one_line: view that category-selective neurons code intrinsic features of isolated categories

frameworks.name: contextual / statistical regularity coding
frameworks.one_line: neurons are sensitive to object relationships reflecting environmental statistics

core_question: Do category-selective neurons in primate object pathway code categories in isolation, or are they modulated by contextual cues from statistical regularities?
key_assumptions: category-selective regions exist for faces, bodies, hands, objects, scenes
experienced environment imparts statistical regularities about object co-occurrence
direct single-neuron recording reveals stimulus selectivity
manipulations: presence vs absence of contextual cues (e.g. body) indicating expected face location
actual face vs non-face content at the contextually-cued location
key_findings: face-selective neurons respond to images of faces
face-selective neurons also respond to image parts where context (body) implies a face should be
this response occurs even when no face is actually present at that location
interpretations: category-selective neurons do not code categories in isolation
they are sensitive to object relationships reflecting statistical regularities of the environment
